title: The Biggest Forex Trade Of My Career

transcript_segments.text: My name is Nick and if you don't know
me, I've been trading for the past
decade in Forex and crypto and stocks.
I've made millions of dollars in all
markets combined. And two days ago, I
had the biggest trade of my entire Forex
trading career in a day. So, I have it
right here. This is my free Telegram
channel. It's where I share all my
trades and analysis. And you can see
right here, I made $150,000
after swaps and commissions. $134,000
basically in a single day from this
trade playing out. I took this trade on
Monday and then I closed this trade on
Wednesday. It was a simple sell at
resistance and price dropped. And I'm
going to break down this trade here in
today's video because of how big of a
deal this trade is for me. It was super
nice. It was super sweet. I shared the
analysis. I shared everything the whole
duration of time for this trade in my
Telegram. I'll put a link in the
description uh in the pin comment if you
want to join my free Telegram channel.
It's free. You can click the link, join
in like five seconds, and you can follow
my daily trades and analysis. So, what I
did on USDJPY, I went into the Trading
View replay mode, and I forwarded this
back to like a couple days ago, so a
couple days before the trade setup. And
I want to show you guys my thought
process because I see a lot of people

matter. And they're doing all these
things at the cost of things that really
do matter, such as keeping things simple
and controlling risk. So, the way that I
trade is very, very simple. You'll

You'll notice the candlesticks are all

indicators. There's no sort of price

that. Um, it's just a plain black and
white charts. And the way that I trade
is I go to the chart and look at where
current market prices. So, in this case,
it was right here on this day. And then
what I'll do is I'll find the most clear
support resistance zones above and below
price on the daily chart. So, I use the
daily and the weekly, but most of my

go to the daily, I don't zoom out really

six months of price data because that's

stop at one of the zones, even
temporarily, and bounce most likely. And
so, it's very simple to draw these
zones. So, I look at current market
price and I find the next clear zone
above price is up here around this area
because it's super super clear that
price bounced right here as resistance.
And then here again as resistance, it

pair couple days ago. The next clear
zone below price is down here around
these lows. So then what I do is I set
two alerts. I set one alert at the next

This is where I elected to close the

and I'm risking 50,000, I'll try to cut

Sometimes it's more, sometimes it even
hits the stop loss, right? One out of
every four or five trades actually goes
and hits the stop loss, and I take the
full unit of loss. Um, but whenever

those trades to the profit target of 1:1
risk-to-reward ratio. And this allows me
to have full take profits hit when I'm
right, and that's roughly half the time,
right? Maybe 45%, 55%, something like
that. just was roughly half the time and
I make more and then whenever I lose the
other half the time I lose less and
that's the only way I'm profitable I
only make anywhere from one to two units
of reward profit per month. So that
means if I'm risking 50k per trade and I
come out at the end of the month I have
to say I've taken like 10 trades or so.
Um you know I win some I lose some but
the conglomerate of my winners generally
outweighs my losses most months out of
the year and I'll come out in one unit
of profit. And in that case that would
be $50,000 profit for the month. In
other cases, right, say I come out in
two units of profit. Um, if I'm risking
an average of 50K per trade, then I'll
come out in $100,000 profit on the
month. And if I get to the end of the
year and I can do any anywhere from like
15 to 30 R cumulatively, that is an
amazing year for me because I'm risking
such high dollar amounts. And I feel
like a lot of people really get the
wrong idea because they think you need
to have really big risk-to-reward
ratios. people are trading small
accounts expecting to grow an account
from $100 to $100,000 over the course
of, you know, a couple weeks or a couple
months or even a year. I think a lot of
that's just unrealistic and I think it
really damages a trader's confidence
because you're able to get into trades
thinking that you're going to be able to
grow the account so much when that's
just not the reality. If you keep things
really, really simple, you just buy and
sell at support and resistance zones in
the higher time frames and then you
manage them by getting out a little
earlier when you're wrong and then
holding them a little longer when you're
right, you end up being able to make so
much more money as compared to everybody
else that's doing the complete opposite.
They're holding their winners too long.
They're closing their losses too long
and they're closing their winners way
too early. And so their average loss
outweighs their average win. When you
pair that with a win rate that's like

what a lot of high-risisk reward systems

like one unit of risk to make two or
three units of profit or more. That
makes it very very difficult for people
to be able to be profitable long term.
And so I believe 1:1 riskreward ratios

efficiency and profitability.
